comments.body:
Fixes #1192 

`tmpfs` support is still required to be added.

If possible, please review if in general this approach is fine. 
- Few logic are common in  `--volume` & `--mount` case. But modifying those functions will be bigger change, so added new functions. Maybe can be optimized in separate PR. 

//cc @rhatdan

Missing changes to:
         Man Pages
         Command Completions.
         Need tests

You need to check what happens if I have existing mounts that you are mounting over, those should be removed from the spec file.

For examples

podman run -v /var/lib/mariadb:/var/lib/mariadb --mount type=bind,src=/var/lib/mariadb,dest=/var/lib/mariadb ...

Or better yet

podman run  --mount type=bind,src=/etc/resolv.conf,dest=/etc/resolv.conf ...

Is the following legal syntax?

podman run  --mount type=tmpfs,dest=/dev/shm ...
@mheon @baude @umohnani8 PTAL
@kunalkushwaha Looks like your tests are failing.

I have updated ``-mount`` flag which can handle both `tmpfs` & `bind`.
- Few of details syntax like
   - source can be specified as `src` or `source`
   - destination can be specified as `dst`, `target` or `destination`

- Mount precedence.
   - image config mounts   <  `-v`  <  `--mount`
      i.e. existing image config mount will be ignored if mount points are provided by `-v` and `--mount` option will overwrite any mount points if provided by `-v` & image config.

- Test cases are added, though many more conditions (test cases) can still be added.

- Updated the `podman-run.1.md` file for man page. 
     - How to generate man page using this?
I still don't like the fact we are parsing the mount string twice.  Can't you consolodate them some how?

> I still don't like the fact we are parsing the mount string twice. Can't you consolodate them some how?

It can done by storing the [`Mounts`](https://github.com/containers/libpod/pull/1432/files#diff-cebb6d9e2dd46ffe9665b195619c5c13R127) information in  `spec.Mount` struct instead of `string`. I used string to just keep the consistency with `Volumes` & `VolumesFrom` elements.
``` patch

+	Mounts             []string              //mounts
	Volumes            []string              //volume
	VolumesFrom        []string
```

I have updated the PR with mount string parsed once.

*PS: I may not able to fix new review comments before Oct.
 I will be on leave for 2 weeks from tomorrow.*
bot, retest this please

@kunalkushwaha Great work.  Only issue I see is that the default should be rprivate not private.  @mheon just changed all default volume mounts to rprivate to prevent some weird leaks.  If you can't fix that now, I will grab the PR and add it.

Don't know why the full test suite did not run.
@rhatdan I am still there. Will update it now.
I think still only _travis-ci_ is only invoked, not others.
papr must be down.
@mheon @baude @umohnani8 @giuseppe @vrothberg  PTAL
:umbrella: The latest upstream changes (presumably #1467) made this pull request unmergeable. Please resolve the merge conflicts.
@kunalkushwaha Could you rebase,  I would love to get this in this week.

Sorry it won't b possible to me to work before Oct 1st as travelling and
don't have stable internet and laptop to work.

Please feel free to update it is it's possible.

Regards,
Kunal Kushwaha
